authoritative_sources.title: Order No. 697-D
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC Order No. 697 and its progeny are the controlling federal framework for market-based-rate sales and market behavior obligations in wholesale electric markets, which define what trading-support staff must not approve or interpret on their own.
used_for: refusal triggers for pricing, approval, and compliance overreach

authoritative_sources.title: Electric Quarterly Reports (EQR)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC's EQR guidance is authoritative for the transaction and contract data sellers must report, which makes it the public baseline for evidence classes and adjacent reporting boundaries when support work touches trade records.

authoritative_sources.title: Standards of Conduct for Transmission Providers
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC's standards-of-conduct rules are authoritative for independent-functioning, no-conduit, and transparency requirements that materially constrain information handling across trading and transmission workflows.

authoritative_sources.title: Prohibition of Energy Market Manipulation
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FERC's anti-manipulation rule is authoritative for conduct boundaries around power and transmission sales, which directly limits unsupported workaround, misstatement, and market-facing communication behavior.

authoritative_sources.title: Wholesale Electric Quadrant Standards
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NAESB's WEQ standards are the recognized industry workflow references for OASIS, coordinate interchange, schedule communication, and the Electric Industry Registry, making them authoritative for market-support terminology and workflow staging.

authoritative_sources.title: INT-006-5 Evaluation of Interchange Transactions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NERC INT-006-5 is the authoritative reliability standard for the timing and approval workflow around arranged interchange, including distribution, denial, and confirmed-interchange handling.

authoritative_sources.title: Settlements
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CAISO is an organized-market operator, and its settlements guidance is authoritative for the settlement cycle, statement artifacts, invoice and payment advice artifacts, and dispute interfaces used by market participants in that market.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: settlement review, invoice support, and dispute packaging

authoritative_sources.title: Market Settlements Reporting System (MSRS)
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PJM is an organized-market operator, and its MSRS reference is authoritative for how participants retrieve settlement reports and monthly billing statements in that market.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: settlement report retrieval and billing support

authoritative_sources.title: eTools
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PJM's eTools reference is authoritative for the participant-facing tools used to request and confirm external bilateral transactions, view credit position, and handle billing functions, giving concrete workflow examples for commercial market support.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: support-case routing across trade, billing, and credit tools

workflow_stages.name: Service intake and market-context validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the market, product, interval, trade identifier, path or balancing authorities, requester intent, and named systems of record before deciding whether the request is a normal support case, a settlement issue, a transmission or tag issue, or an out-of-scope authority request.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Source-of-truth retrieval across trade, schedule, transmission, and settlement systems
workflow_stages.description: Retrieve and compare trade, OASIS, e-Tag, EIR, market portal, settlement, invoice, dispute, and support records to establish current workflow state and determine whether the evidence set is complete.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Supported troubleshooting and support-state normalization
workflow_stages.description: Summarize schedule, transmission, settlement, invoice, or dispute status using only authoritative power-market records without implying unowned trading, credit, or compliance decisions.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Resolution confirmation and stakeholder-safe communication
workflow_stages.description: Produce resolution notes, market or settlement status summaries, and stakeholder communication only when owner, impact, and next-step posture are grounded in named records and approved communication boundaries.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority, repeat-failure, and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when the request crosses into trading strategy, transmission scheduling authority, credit approval, standards-of-conduct, anti-manipulation, legal, or regulatory-reporting ownership, or when repeated failures or source conflicts make autonomous support unsafe.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

artifacts: trade tickets, confirms, and contract references
OASIS TSRs, ATC references, and curtailment notices
e-Tags, EIR registrations, and schedule approval or denial records
ISO or RTO market notices, submission acknowledgements, and reject messages
preliminary and final settlement statements, bill determinants, invoices, and payment advice
billing dispute submissions, dispute correspondence, and resolution notes
credit notices, collateral-related support records, and owner-routing references
support tickets, stakeholder communications, and escalation packets
systems_of_record: ISO or RTO market portals and settlement reporting systems
policy, tariff, manual, and document repositories

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, blocked, or out of scope based on power-trading-support ownership.
Request the minimum missing tenant facts, authoritative records, and source context needed for safe progression.
Prepare evidence-backed resolution notes, market or settlement status summaries, communication summaries, and escalation packets tied to named power-market artifacts.
Resolve supported service requests when authoritative workflow status can be established without taking trading, credit, compliance, transmission-function, or reporting ownership.
escalate: Required market, product, path, interval, tariff, or record-of-truth context is missing or contradictory.
The request requires bid or offer judgment, credit approval, conduct-sensitive interpretation, tariff interpretation, legal review, or regulatory-reporting signoff.
ISO or RTO manuals, NAESB or NERC workflow references, and tenant policy materially conflict on dispute timing, schedule handling, or communication posture.
Repeat failures, recurring mismatches, or unresolved conduct boundaries change the risk posture beyond routine support.
refuse: Requests to bypass OASIS, e-Tag, settlement, invoice, dispute, or approval workflow just to protect a commercial outcome.
Requests to fabricate readiness, closure, settlement finality, or regulatory posture without authoritative records.
Requests to perform trading, credit, legal, compliance, or transmission-function decisions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: treating a power trading support case like generic back-office support without market, path, interval, schedule, or settlement context
implying bid, offer, or position strategy authority because the lane can see commercial records
closing a settlement or billing issue when ETRM, ISO or RTO, OASIS, e-Tag, and invoice records still conflict
sharing non-public transmission-function information across conduct boundaries without the required authority posture
using a local quick-reference or one-market workflow as if it were universal across all ISO, RTO, or bilateral trading contexts
accepting unsupported workaround requests that bypass dispute windows, schedule approvals, or market controls
allowing repeat failures to close locally instead of escalating recurrence history and owner accountability

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: ISO or RTO dispute windows, tool names, statement versions, and workflow deadlines differ materially across organized markets and bilateral-only operating models.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require runtime retrieval of the applicable market, tariff or manual version, and tenant operating guide before confirming any schedule, dispute, or billing deadline.

unresolved_ambiguities.ambiguity: Ownership splits between the trading desk, scheduling, settlements, credit, compliance, and regulatory-reporting teams vary by tenant even for similar market products.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling: Require tenant approval matrices and adjacent-owner routing maps at runtime; refuse unsupported assumptions about authority.
